title: Distinguish stray req labels from unsatisfied ones
description:
Distinguish a `req:` label whose id does not appear in the canonical criteria section (stray or invented) from one that is declared but not yet satisfied by a child, so the two failure modes are separable by an agent.

## Background

The label-reference rule compares label values only against other labels, never against ids extracted from the criteria prose. A fabricated `req:REQ-77` and a legitimate not-yet-implemented `req:REQ-01` produce the identical rule, message, and severity.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A check (new rule kind or an extension of the existing graph kinds) compares `req:`-namespace label values against criterion ids extracted from the configured criteria section
- [ ] A label with no matching criterion id yields a finding that names the unmatched id and the section searched
- [ ] A declared-but-unsatisfied label yields a clearly different finding text
- [ ] Unit tests cover both paths plus the id-format-mismatch case (criterion `REQ-03` vs label `req:REQ-3`)
